2603.21676.title: Thinking Deeper, Not Longer: Depth-Recurrent Transformers for Compositional Generalization
2603.21676.paper_summary: Standard Transformers have a fixed computational depth, fundamentally limiting their ability to generalize to tasks requiring variable-depth reasoning, such as multi-hop graph traversal or nested logic. We propose a depth-recurrent Transformer that decouples computational depth from parameter count by iteratively applying a shared-weight Transformer block in latent space -- enabling the model to trade recurrence steps for deeper reasoning at inference time. Our architecture incorporates three mechanisms to make deep recurrence (20+ steps) stable: (1) a silent thinking objective that supervises only the final output, forcing genuine multi-step reasoning rather than intermediate heuristic shortcuts; (2) LayerScale initialization to protect fragile reasoning states from untrained layer noise; and (3) an identity-biased recurrence that creates a gradient highway across many steps. We evaluate on three compositional reasoning domains with decreasing inductive biases: graph reachability (strict adjacency masking), nested boolean logic (relative positioning), and unstructured relational text (where sequence position provides no structural hints). Across all tasks, we observe a clear \emph{computational frontier} -- a boundary where performance transitions from chance to near-perfect as thinking steps scale with task complexity. Moreover, these tasks reveal qualitatively different generalization behaviors: precise but brittle (graph), approximate but robust (logic), and autonomous latent routing without structural hints (text). This progression illuminates how the interplay between a task-invariant recurrent reasoning core and task-specific perceptual interfaces shapes out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization, offering a mechanistic perspective on vertical chain-of-thought that complements the prevailing horizontal token-generation paradigm.
2603.21676.paper_summary_zh: 標準Transformer具有固定的計算深度，根本限制了它們在需要變化深度推理的任務中的泛化能力，例如多跳圖遍歷或嵌套邏輯。我們提出了一種深度重複Transformer，通過在潛在空間中迭代應用共享權重的Transformer塊，將計算深度與參數數量解耦——使模型能夠在推理時用重複步驟換取更深的推理。我們的架構包含三個機制，以使深度重複（20+ 步驟）保持穩定：（1）一個靜默思考目標，僅監督最終輸出，迫使真正的多步推理，而不是中間的啟發式捷徑；（2）LayerScale 初始化，以保護脆弱的推理狀態免受未訓練層噪聲的影響；以及（3）一種偏向恆等的重複，創造了一條跨越多個步驟的梯度高速公路。我們在三個具有遞減歸納偏見的組合推理領域進行評估：圖可達性（嚴格的鄰接掩碼）、嵌套布爾邏輯（相對位置）和無結構關係文本（序列位置不提供結構提示）。在所有任務中，我們觀察到一個明確的 \emph{計算邊界}——一個性能從隨機轉變為接近完美的邊界，隨著任務複雜性的增加而擴展思考步驟。此外，這些任務揭示了質量上不同的泛化行為：精確但脆弱（圖），近似但穩健（邏輯），以及在沒有結構提示的情況下進行自主潛在路由（文本）。這一進程闡明了任務不變的重複推理核心與任務特定的感知介面的相互作用如何塑造分佈外（OOD）泛化，提供了一種機械視角，補充了當前的水平令牌生成範式。
2603.21676.author: Hung-Hsuan Chen et.al.
